sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Asthma – 38
sources.population: People presenting with suspected or established asthma in the populations specified by the guideline.
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for Dubai telehealth asthma documentation; emergency management and controlled-medicine prescribing are excluded and in-person use remains partial.

exact_sections.heading: 2. Scope; 3. Purpose; 4. Applicability
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines DHA telehealth scope, licensed-facility applicability, and emergency and controlled-medication exclusions.

exact_sections.heading: 5. Clinical symptoms and 6. Diagnosis
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports wheeze, cough, breathlessness, chest tightness, variation, nocturnal or seasonal pattern, triggers, exercise and occupational context, atopy or family history, bronchodilator response, respiratory examination, peak flow, and objective confirmation.

exact_sections.heading: 7. Red Flags
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports sudden or severe breathing difficulty, cardiac or chest symptoms, cyanosis, inability to speak full sentences, confusion or agitation, stridor or audible wheeze, immobility or trauma, orthopnoea, status asthmaticus, and near-fatal asthma history as clinician-assessed prompts.

exact_sections.heading: 9. Investigation
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports peak flow, pulmonary function testing, spirometry, bronchodilator reversibility, and selected clinician-led investigations.

exact_sections.heading: 11.8 Uncontrolled asthma and 12. Referral criteria
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports review of alternative or comorbid causes, adherence, inhaler technique, smoking, occupational, psychosocial and environmental factors, treatment response, device review, and clinician-led referral.

sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Cough – 46
sources.population: People presenting with cough; age limits are not stated in the applicability section.
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for Dubai telehealth cough documentation; only partial for a general in-person workflow and for chronic-cough investigation pathways.

exact_sections.heading: 2. Scope; 3. Purpose; 4. Applicability
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines DHA telehealth scope, Dubai purpose, licensed-provider applicability, and emergency and controlled-medication exclusions.

exact_sections.heading: 5. Red Flags
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists cough features that require serious-condition assessment or face-to-face escalation.

exact_sections.heading: 6. Clinical History
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Specifies cough duration, quality, timing, risk factors, and associated-symptom history domains.

exact_sections.heading: 8. Investigations
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-selected blood tests, chest radiography, peak-flow diary, pulmonary function testing, sputum testing, ECG, and specialist-led advanced imaging.

exact_sections.heading: 9. Referral criteria
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led emergency, family-medicine, and pulmonary referral according to the documented cough context.

sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.population: Children, young people, and adults with community- or hospital-acquired pneumonia, subject to recommendation-specific setting and age qualifiers.
sources.clinical_setting: First contact, primary care, hospital, community treatment, reassessment, and post-discharge follow-up.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for selected pneumonia recovery, reassessment, observations, and post-inpatient chest-radiograph fields; international guidance requires UAE clinician review and the legacy workflow does not enforce every age or discharge qualifier.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2 and 1.6.7 plus terms — clinical observations and signs
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-recorded temperature, respiratory rate, oxygen saturation, mental status, eating or hydration context, and focal chest examination findings without asserting severity or diagnosis.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.10.1–1.10.4 — recovery and when to seek further advice
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports review of fever, cough, sputum, breathlessness, chest pain, fatigue or functional recovery, worsening or non-improving symptoms, systemic illness, and clinician-documented safety advice, with adult and child qualifiers preserved.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.11.1–1.11.8 — reassessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports reassessment when symptoms or signs do not improve or worsen, clinician-led referral, microbiology where specified, and CRP or procalcitonin review in the qualified hospital treatment-failure context.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.12.1–1.12.3 — follow-up chest X-rays
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports no routine post-inpatient follow-up radiograph, consideration at 6 weeks for smoking, age over 50, persistent or deteriorating symptoms, or unexplained weight loss, and shared decision-making using recent imaging, comorbidity, frailty, prognosis, treatment options, and preferences.
